name: Origami Risk Management Information System API
what_it_does: Origami Risk RMIS (Risk Management Information System) REST API for enterprise risk management, insurance program management, claims, safety, and compliance. Enables AI agents to manage insurance policy and certificate tracking for enterprise insurance program automation, handle incident and claim intake for risk management workflow automation, access safety observation and near-miss tracking for EHS compliance automation, retrieve workers compensation and liability claim management for self-insured employer automation, manage certificate of insurance collection and verification for vendor risk management, handle actuarial data and loss run generation for insurance program reporting, access policy renewal workflow and broker communication for insurance procurement automation, retrieve risk analytics and benchmarking for enterprise risk reporting, manage captive insurance program administration for self-insured risk financing, and integrate Origami with TPA systems (Gallagher Bassett, Sedgwick), HR platforms, and ERP for end-to-end enterprise risk management.
use_cases: Tracking insurance policies from program management agents
Managing claims from risk department agents
Collecting COIs from vendor compliance agents
Reporting risk analytics from CFO risk agents
not_for: Small business insurance (use commercial insurance brokers)
best_when: An enterprise risk management department, large self-insured employer, or captive insurance program wanting AI agents to automate insurance policy tracking, claim intake, COI collection, safety incident management, and risk analytics within Origami as the enterprise RMIS.
avoid_when: ACTUARIAL RESERVE ACCURACY FOR FINANCIAL REPORTING: Automated actuarial data extraction and reserve adequacy reporting from Origami must be reviewed by qualified actuary (FCAS or ACAS) before financial statement use; automated reserve reporting without actuarial sign-off creates financial statement misrepresentation risk for self-insured programs with SEC disclosure obligations. CERTIFICATE OF INSURANCE VALIDATION COVERAGE ADEQUACY: Automated COI collection via Origami must verify required coverage types, limits, and endorsements against contractual requirements; automated COI acceptance without coverage adequacy verification creates vendor risk transfer gap and potential uninsured contractor exposure. PRIVACY OF WORKERS COMPENSATION CLAIMANT DATA: Automated access to WC claimant data in Origami must be limited to risk management and adjuster roles; automated claimant data access by supervisors or operations managers without state WC law authorization creates worker privacy violation.

auth.notes: Origami Risk uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes. REST API with JSON. Chicago, Illinois HQ. Founded 2009 by Robert Petrie. Private ($200M+ raised, Spectrum Equity, Great Hill Partners). Modern cloud-native RMIS challenging legacy vendors. 1,500+ risk management programs. Enterprise, mid-market, and public sector focus. Claims, safety, insurance, and compliance modules. Highly configurable with low-code workflow. Competes with Ventiv, Riskonnect, and Majesco for enterprise RMIS.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual subscription based on module and user count
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription by module (claims, safety, insurance, compliance) and user count; enterprise and mid-market tiers; implementation professional services
pricing.notes: Chicago IL. Private ($200M+, Spectrum Equity, Great Hill). Founded 2009. 1,500+ risk programs. Cloud RMIS. Annual subscription by module and user.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; claims and analytics queries throttled; webhooks for claim and policy events

known_agent_gotchas: ORIGAMI HIGH CONFIGURABILITY REQUIRES INSTANCE-SPECIFIC SCHEMA KNOWLEDGE: Origami Risk is highly configurable with customer-specific data fields, workflows, and module configurations; automated API integrations must be designed against the specific Origami instance configuration, not generic schema; field names, picklist values, and workflow states differ per customer implementation

COI VERIFICATION COVERAGE ADEQUACY BEYOND CERTIFICATE COLLECTION: Automated COI collection must verify required coverage types, limits, additional insured endorsements, and waiver of subrogation as required by contract; automated COI receipt confirmation without coverage verification creates vendor risk transfer gap; implement coverage adequacy rules engine against contract requirements

ACTUARIAL RESERVE SIGN-OFF FOR FINANCIAL STATEMENT USE: Automated loss run and reserve data export from Origami for financial reporting (SEC, rating agency, audit) requires qualified actuary review and sign-off; automated reserve adequacy reports used in financial statements without FCAS/ACAS actuarial certification creates financial reporting misrepresentation

TPA data integration latency for claims imported from external systems — Origami imports claim data from TPA systems (Gallagher Bassett, Sedgwick); TPA data integration may have daily batch lag vs real-time Origami API data; automated claims analytics using Origami data must account for TPA import batch timing; real-time claim queries may reflect import lag

Incident vs claim workflow distinction — Origami separates incident/near-miss recording (safety module) from claim filing (claims module); automated incident reports must be evaluated for claim potential before automated claim conversion; automated incident-to-claim conversion without adjuster evaluation creates unnecessary claim reservation for non-compensable incidents

Multi-entity and subsidiary insurance program isolation — Origami supports multi-entity risk programs with subsidiary isolation; automated reporting and claim management must correctly scope to authorized entities; automated cross-entity data access without entity-specific authorization creates confidentiality breach for subsidiary programs with different ownership
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise RMIS. SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2. US/EU/AU. Claims, WC claimant, and insurance program data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise RMIS. SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2. US/EU/AU. Claims, insurance, and risk management data.
